narrative: Dear Alex, imagine the admissions team looking at your application like they're piecing together the story of your journey to the bar exam, drawing from patterns they've seen in thousands of students before you. Your Year 3 decile of 1 stood out as the biggest chapter in this story, signaling a significant dip in performance that the model saw as the strongest pull toward struggling on the bar, even after a solid Year 1 decile of 4. Layered on top was your LSAT score of 30, which whispered doubts about the raw analytical firepower needed to conquer those tough exams. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.9 added to that narrative, painting a picture of consistent academic hurdles rather than steady climbs. Coming from a lower-middle family income bracket, the model noted how that background has historically made the intense prep process tougher for others like you. Your Asian background and male gender also nudged the story toward higher risks, based on trends in past outcomes. Even choosing full-time study, while dedicated, didn't shift the tale enough in these eyes. Together, these elements tipped the scales to a 52% chance of not passing the bar, leading to this tough call on your application. It's not about your potential or heart—it's just the mosaic they saw. If you boost that late-game academic momentum or retake the LSAT, the story could rewrite itself entirely. You've got this drive; channel it wisely. Keep pushing forward.
